name: Fruit Cake (an Old German Recipe)
recipeInstructions:
Reserve 1 tablespoon butter for frying nuts
Bleached small white raisins are preferable to puffed, seeded ones.
Gum drops should be cut more easily if scissors are first dipped into hot water.
Use only a few green ones as their flavor is quite strong; no black.
1. Cream together the sugar and butter. Add the slightly beaten eggs, then mix in the applesauce and vanilla.
2. Sift the remaining 6 dry ingredients through a fine mesh sieve into a separate bowl.
3. Reserve 1 cup of this sifted mixture for later use.
4. Gradually add the dry ingredients (excluding the reserved cup) to the creamed mixture, mixing well.
5. Fry the nuts in the reserved butter until they are heated and lightly browned but not overcooked; stir constantly.
6. Combine the chopped nuts, gumdrops, and raisins with the remaining sifted flour.
7. Line cake pans with wax paper, ensuring they are no more than two-thirds full when filled.
8. Bake in a slow oven (275°F) until done. The baking time depends on the size of the cakes; an 8½ x 4-inch loaf requires about 2 hours. Do not overbake; the cake should be moist inside.
9. Remove the wax paper immediately after taking the cake out of the oven.
10. Wrap the entire cake completely in fresh wax paper or cellophane to seal in moisture. The paper used for baking will harden and become difficult to remove later when cold.
This recipe yields approximately one 8½ x 4-inch loaf cake and one 8-inch square cake, which can keep indefinitely.
